title: Mirror subitem column from one board to another board item
excerpt:
Hello, 
I’m setting up monday in my workplace (Design agency), and I currently have a board for the clients and for New requests. Each group is a different client, and within the group, each item is a different project, …

posts.author: MariaM1 (Maria)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello,

I’m setting up monday in my workplace (Design agency), and I currently have a board for the clients and for New requests. Each group is a different client, and within the group, each item is a different project, each subitem is a different product of a projects that we need to develop.

I have this Client board connected to a Request board and I’ve mirrored the clients and projects names, so Designers know what they are project working on and for who, however, I can’t mirror the subitems, so they don’t know what product the task assigned is for. Is there a way I can mirror the subitems column of the Clients board to the Request board item? I couldn’t make this work

The other way I thought was creating a dropdown in the Client board for the products to be developed, however each project has a different list of products, and we get more than 300 projects/year with multiple type of products, so I think the list will be get too long.

I am completely out of ideas how to make this work. I appreciate all the help, thank you :)!

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
@MariaM1

Am I understanding correctly that you want to create a connect boards column from the subitem of the Clients board to the main items of the Request board?

Jim - The Monday Man
Get Custom Apps, Integrations & Automations for monday

posts.author: MariaM1 (Maria)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Jim, yes, you are correct :)! Is this possible somehow? I’ve been learning a lot about the platform, but a few things I couldn’t figure out yet

Thank you so much!

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
Okay. You can create this from the Clients board. Just add the connect board column to the subitems. If you want the connection to go the other way, make sure to check the “Create a two-way connection” box when adding the board to the column.

Jim - The Monday Man
Get Custom Apps, Integrations & Automations for monday

posts.author: sirah (Sirah E)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Maria,

Sirah here from Mindflows - it is possible to mirror a subitem to a parent item in another board. Have you tried adding a Connect Board Column onto the subitem in your Clients Board then a mirror column on the Request Board?

Hope this helps!

posts.author: MariaM1 (Maria)
posts.cookedHtml: Jim, thank you so so so much!! I think I was doing something wrong, but it did work!!

posts.author: MariaM1 (Maria)
posts.cookedHtml: Sirah, thank you!! :)) It worked!! I appreciate it!

posts.author: sirah (Sirah E)
posts.cookedHtml: Glad this worked for you! Happy to help!
